q: The magnitude of the charge on each conductor, $Q$, is called the _____ of the capacitor.
The quantity of charge $Q$ on the capacitor is linearly proportional to the _____ $V$ between the conductors.
In the relationship $Q = CV$, $C$ is called the _____ of the capacitor.
In the SI system, the unit of capacitance is _____.
Typical devices have capacitances ranging from microfarad ($\mu\text{F}$) to _____.
Although the capacitance of a capacitor does not depend on $Q$ and $V$, it depends on the size and the _____ of the capacitor.
The capacitance also depends on the nature of the _____ between the two conductors.
The SI unit of electric capacity is also _____.
